intro: Amalgam (silver) fillings contain mercury. We help you understand what that means for your health and what options you have—including safe removal when treatment is indicated.
videoCaption: The International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology (IAOMT) has long studied mercury exposure from dental amalgam. Dr. Clark discontinued placing amalgam in 1985 and follows mercury-safe protocols when removal is planned.

amalgamReplacement.title: Global health policies
amalgamReplacement.body: Many health authorities have tightened guidance on dental amalgam—particularly for children and during pregnancy—under international mercury-reduction frameworks such as the Minamata Convention. In the European Union, several countries have phased down or restricted amalgam use in line with those goals. Regulations continue to evolve; we focus on informed consent, safe materials, and careful technique when replacing or removing existing amalgam.

seo.title: Mercury Fillings & Safe Removal | Amity Gentle Dental
seo.description: Mercury amalgam fillings, IAOMT-informed perspective, and mercury-safe dentistry with Dr. Chester V. Clark Jr. in Amity, AR.
